boundedInputSummary: Bounded phase0 hotel phone-loss input is replayed through persisted retrieval, validator, and review artifacts before entering the demo-side projection.

flowSteps.label: Bounded input manifest
flowSteps.summary: Bounded phase0 hotel phone-loss input is replayed through persisted retrieval, validator, and review artifacts before entering the demo-side projection.
flowSteps.note: No fresh live retrieval is required for this wave; the bounded input is replayed locally against persisted artifacts.

flowSteps.label: Retrieval contour anchor
flowSteps.summary: 2 persisted evidence items survived retrieval filtering.
flowSteps.note: This is the runtime source for the phase0 evidence bundle.

flowSteps.label: Validator contour anchor
flowSteps.summary: 2 validator-visible signals were emitted from the persisted evidence bundle.
flowSteps.note: Validator output remains directly inspectable before the review gate tightens posture.

flowSteps.label: Review contour anchor
flowSteps.summary: 2 reviewed signals remain operator-visible with conservative hold decisions.
flowSteps.note: This is the chosen primary operator-facing run for the demo wave.

flowSteps.label: Read-side projection bundle
flowSteps.summary: Generated read-side JSON plus explicit demo-only lineage/provenance overlays feed the UI without mutating runtime or governance.
flowSteps.note: The generator rebuilds the UI-readable bundle from tracked artifacts, but the overview/provenance layer is still a demo-side stitch.

flowSteps.label: Operator UI surface
flowSteps.summary: Inbox, detail, comparison, and feedback routes all read the generated bundle and keep provenance/state semantics explicit.
flowSteps.note: The UI is intentionally demo-scoped and does not open a production contour.

flowSteps.label: Feedback mock boundary
flowSteps.summary: Feedback stays explicitly local and demo-only, so the operator surface never mutates runtime or governance automatically.
flowSteps.note: Mock is a deliberate visible boundary, not a hidden implementation gap.

materializationLegend.label: artifact_backed
materializationLegend.meaning: The visible object is anchored in repo-tracked runtime artifacts and can be traced back to specific manifests, signal rows, or evidence records.

materializationLegend.label: hybrid
materializationLegend.meaning: The visible object is rebuilt from tracked artifacts plus demo-side stitching or draft-derived mapping that remains intentionally explicit.

materializationLegend.label: mock
materializationLegend.meaning: The visible object is demo-only and stays outside runtime and governance mutation paths.

runStateLegend.label: populated
runStateLegend.meaning: The run has surfaced operator-visible signal rows with inspectable evidence traces.

runStateLegend.label: blocked
runStateLegend.meaning: The contour stopped before a fair signal-emitting stage completed and the UI keeps that blockage explicit.

runStateLegend.label: zero_signal
runStateLegend.meaning: The contour completed far enough to produce a run object, but it emitted zero operator-visible signals.

runStateLegend.label: inconclusive
runStateLegend.meaning: The contour surfaced a partial signal view, but the evidence remained too thin for a stronger conclusion.

assumptions: phase0-review-run-001 remains the primary artifact-backed operator anchor for this demo wave.
The new end_to_end_demo manifests stay demo-only and do not redefine decision_read_model_contract_v1.
phase0-smoke-run-001, a2-live-gate-001, and a2-live-gate-002 remain secondary honesty states rather than the primary walkthrough path.
completionCriteria: The projection/build command regenerates both the base demo read model and the explicit end_to_end_demo bundle.
The Run Inbox shows bounded input summary, chosen run/artifact anchor, materialization legend, and state legend.
The chosen run detail shows visible run lineage plus signal lineage with state, materialization_mode, source_artifact_refs, and projection_notes.
The comparison route shows explicit hybrid projection context instead of implying a fully canonical comparator.
The feedback route keeps mock persistence explicit and does not hide the write-side boundary.
